assertion_results.text: Version impact table shows mixed results: 2.1.x versions marked YES (affected) and 2.2.x versions marked NO (not affected) based on lock file evidence
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md contains a table with 2.1.0 and 2.1.1 marked YES (h2 0.4.5 < 0.4.8) and all 2.2.x versions (2.2.0 through 2.2.4) marked NO (h2 >= 0.4.8). The summary explicitly states: '2.1.x stream: ALL versions affected' and '2.2.x stream: NO versions affected' and 'This is a mixed impact scenario.'

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction includes only the actually affected versions (2.1.x versions), not the unaffected 2.2.x versions (Step 3.2)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md proposes changing Affects Versions from [RHTPA 2.1.0, RHTPA 2.2.0] to [RHTPA 2.1.0, RHTPA 2.1.1]. The Jira mutation explicitly sets versions to only 2.1.x: '{"versions": [{"name": "RHTPA 2.1.0"}, {"name": "RHTPA 2.1.1"}]}'. RHTPA 2.2.0 is explicitly removed with explanation: 'lock file at v0.4.5 shows h2 0.4.8 (fixed version, not affected).'

assertion_results.text: Remediation tasks are created only for the affected stream (2.1.x), not for 2.2.x which already ships the patched version (Step 7 Case A scoping)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md creates exactly two tasks, both scoped to 2.1.x: Task 1 targets release/0.3.z (2.1.x upstream backport) and Task 2 targets rhtpa-release.0.3.z (2.1.x downstream propagation). The file explicitly states: 'only the 2.1.x stream is affected. The 2.2.x stream ships h2 >= 0.4.8 (the fixed version) in all releases and requires no remediation.' No tasks reference the 2.2.x stream or release/0.4.z.

assertion_results.text: The analysis treats the issue as unscoped (no stream suffix) and checks ALL versions across all streams (Step 1 Stream scope resolution, unscoped path)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md states: 'The issue summary... contains no stream suffix in brackets. Therefore this issue is unscoped and covers all configured version streams.' It then lists both streams: '2.1.x -- Konflux release repo rhtpa-release.0.3.z' and '2.2.x -- Konflux release repo rhtpa-release.0.4.z'. version-impact.md checks all 7 versions across both streams (2.1.0, 2.1.1, 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4).

assertion_results.text: Cross-stream impact notice is NOT generated because the issue is unscoped — it covers all streams by definition (Step 7 Case B applies only to scoped issues)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md explicitly states: 'No cross-stream impact notice is generated because the issue is unscoped (it covers all streams by definition, so there is no "other stream outside this issue's scope").' No cross-stream impact notice appears anywhere in any of the output files.
